questions.question: What is "Denial of Wallet"?
options: A provider refusing to issue API credits
An autonomous agent that keeps reasoning, tool-calling, and billing inside a loop because nothing was designed to stop it
A user disputing a charge
A rate limit that blocks legitimate traffic
questions.explanation: A chatbot's bad output is a bad reply; an agent's bad loop is a bill. The documented e-commerce case tripled monthly cost ($1,200 → $4,800) after a new order-tracking tool let the agent poll on every session with no per-tool cap.

questions.question: Why is a single monthly budget cap insufficient?
options: Monthly caps are too expensive to compute
Different failure modes need different time scales — a monthly cap catches a runaway loop only after the wallet is gone; velocity limits catch loops in minutes
Providers don't support monthly billing
Monthly caps violate the EU AI Act
questions.explanation: The stack spans scales: velocity limits catch runaway loops (minutes), daily caps catch slow leaks (hours), monthly caps catch a bad release (days). A single cap at any one scale misses the others.

questions.question: What does a financial velocity limit do that an iteration cap does not?
options: Nothing — they are identical
It trips on spend rate (e.g. >$50 in 10 minutes), catching a token-cheap-but-frequent polling loop before slower caps fire
It limits the number of users
It caps the model's parameter count
questions.explanation: A loop can stay under any per-request cap and under a daily total while burning fast. Velocity limits watch the rate, firing within the loop's own time window — the layer that catches burn before monthly caps even notice.

questions.question: Why does Anthropic frame Auto Mode as requiring budget controls specifically?
options: Budgets make the classifier more accurate
The safety classifier is orthogonal to cost — an autoMode session without max_budget_usd is ungoverned autonomy, since a 'safe' action can still loop expensively
Budgets are required to enable Auto Mode in the UI
Auto Mode disables all other caps
questions.explanation: The classifier judges safety, not spend. A trajectory of individually-safe actions can still burn the wallet, so max_turns and max_budget_usd are mandatory companions to the permission classifier, not alternatives.

questions.question: What is the operational template from the $1,200 → $4,800 case?
options: Cap total users per month
Every new tool surface is a new potential loop — so every new tool needs its own per-tool cap and its own growth alert
Disable all tools by default
Switch to a cheaper provider when costs rise
questions.explanation: The fix was a per-tool cap plus a daily-growth alert. The general rule: adding a tool adds a loop surface, and the governance stack must extend to cover it — matching OWASP Agentic Top 10 and EU AI Act Article 14 oversight requirements.
